leagueAdjustment: TheLeague uses tiered PPR (TE 1.0, WR 0.5, RB 0.25) and 6pt passing TDs -- QBs worth more, WRs/RBs worth less for receptions vs RSP baseline
shelfLife: 3 years from draft class (expires after 2028 season)
shelfLifeStatus: Pre-draft -- awaiting NFL Draft for landing spots and post-draft adjustments

waldmanOverview.qb: Fernando Mendoza is a top-5 overall pick regardless of format. He and Caleb Williams have nearly identical grades. Not the year to target a QB to build around unless you get Mendoza. Wait on QBs until rounds 3-5 if Mendoza is gone. WRs and premium TEs are the priority.
waldmanOverview.rb: Consensus view is this class is bad, but Waldman disagrees -- 5 of his top 15 backs didn't get Combine invites. Jeremiyah Love has All-Pro potential. Next 6-8 have Pro Bowl potential with ideal fit. Multiple sleeper talents below NFL radar. Play it safe and stockpile receivers, pick spots at RB after round 2-3. Consider trading early picks for future capital in 2027-28.
waldmanOverview.wr: Priority position this year. Deep class with Carnell Tate as the franchise-caliber top talent (92.3 DoT score). Strong depth through the first 20+ receivers. Several boom-bust profiles require careful evaluation of risk tolerance.
waldmanOverview.te: 8-10 TEs worth considering in PPR formats. First 3 have high production upside, 6-8 could become starters. Potential offensive trend toward big blocking TEs may phase out move TEs unless they're elite pass-catchers. Eli Stowers is TE1 but Trigg and Sadiq have more athletic upside.

A.description: See field early, develop into productive starters within 2-3 years

B.description: Short-term depth obstacle but starter talent; contributor talent with immediate fit

C.description: Contributor potential on crowded depth chart; situational talent with good immediate fit

D.description: Stash -- long-term developmental talent or reserve

F.description: UDFAs watched but not formally scored -- bump 35-50 spots if signed

typeSymbols.Z: Sleeper -- lesser-known talent with skills if earns opportunity

valueSystem.Under X: RSP values player X picks higher than ADP (bargain -- add X to rank for latest you can get him)
valueSystem.Over X: RSP values player X picks lower than ADP (market overvalues -- subtract X from rank for where others take him)
valueSystem.WW: Waiver wire / undrafted in fantasy leagues

gradeScale.Starter: 85-89.9 -- Starting immediately with large role and learning on the go
gradeScale.Rotational Starter: 80-84.9 -- Executes at a starter level in a role that plays to their strengths
gradeScale.Contributor: 75-79.9 -- Starter execution in a limited role; diminishing returns beyond that scope
gradeScale.Reserve: 70-74.9 -- Contributor with limitations in scope and execution
gradeScale.Developmental: 65-69.9 -- Special teams, end of bench, practice squad

players.name: Fernando Mendoza
players.comparison: Andrew Luck/Justin Herbert - Drake Maye - Blaine Gabbert
players.notes: Top overall prospect. RSP QB1. Smart, aggressive, accurate downfield creator who can maneuver a pocket. One of the best QBs Waldman has scouted in 5-7 years. Nearly identical grade to Caleb Williams though different styles. Mendoza is a mashup of Andrew Luck and Justin Herbert. Accurate to 55-60 yards. Top 5 selection regardless of format. 6pt passing TDs in TheLeague makes him even more valuable than RSP baseline suggests.
players.fantasyAdvice: Top five selection in this draft regardless of format. Arguably the top selection in a dynasty format.

players.name: Ty Simpson
players.comparison: Bo Nix - Colt McCoy - Shea Patterson
players.notes: Distant No.2 on RSP board. Has as much potential as any QB from 2025 class. Good enough to start and thrive with strong surrounding talent. Lessons to learn with craft -- expect early insertion and thriving while defenses study his game. On cusp of Rotational Starter tier.
players.fantasyAdvice: Second-round value in one-QB drafts and mid-to-late first-round option in Super Flex.

players.name: Garrett Nussmeier
players.comparison: Drew Brees - Brock Purdy - Drew Lock - Zach Wilson
players.notes: Volatile boom-bust talent in this QB class. Mobile with arm talent. Played hurt, endured dysfunctional environment. Aggressive risk-taker with good arm. Could emerge as capable starter in 1-2 years.
players.fantasyAdvice: Mid-round value in rookie drafts whose value might increase if an NFL team falls in love with the pre-draft process.

players.name: Cade Klubnik
players.comparison: Kirk Cousins - Tyson Bagent - Shane Buechele
players.notes: Nationally acclaimed high school prospect who took a promising development path at Clemson. Fourth QB could surprise if he addresses certain weaknesses with accuracy and arm talent.
players.fantasyAdvice: Late-round pick in Super Flex formats or one-QB leagues with large rosters and practice squads.

players.name: Diego Pavia
players.comparison: Baker Mayfield/Russell Wilson - Doug Flutie
players.notes: Frankenstein QB experiment -- Baker Mayfield's attitude, Russell Wilson mobility. By far the most accurate intermediate thrower in this class. Pavia's intermediate accuracy was well above standard minimum on pinpoint passes alone.
players.fantasyAdvice: End of draft stash in Super Flex formats or large roster leagues.

players.name: Taylen Green
players.comparison: Colin Kaepernick - Terrelle Pryor
players.notes: High ceiling athletic QB. Second-tier QB after Mendoza who is worth monitoring.
players.fantasyAdvice: Second-tier QB after Mendoza. Worth a look in Super Flex or large roster formats.

players.name: Carson Beck
players.comparison: C.J. Stroud - Derek Carr
players.notes: NFL will covet Beck more than initial press. Top prospect by some. On cusp of Rotational Starter. Fantasy value could increase if NFL gives him first-round draft capital. Based on what RSP has seen, he's a mid-range QB prospect.
players.fantasyAdvice: Fantasy value could increase if NFL gives him first-round draft capital.

players.name: Cole Payton
players.comparison: Dak Prescott
players.notes: Enticing athlete with good speed and strong acceleration. Hurdles defenders in the open field.
players.fantasyAdvice: Monitor from afar.

players.name: Jalon Daniels
players.comparison: Jalen Milroe - Malik Willis
players.notes: Performances on tape are a series of promising fragments not stitched together into a cohesive whole.
players.fantasyAdvice: In Super Flex with practice squads, worth consideration in late rounds of large leagues.

players.name: Luke Altmyer
players.comparison: Garrett Nussmeier - C.J. Beathard
players.notes: Runs an offense with many NFL elements. Takes snaps from center, uses full complement of drops.
players.fantasyAdvice: Monitor from afar. Consider for practice squad in large leagues if good training camp.

players.name: Jack Strand
players.comparison: Kyle Trask - Nate Stanley
players.notes: Internet buzz proclaimed him the best QB in the class. Ranking says otherwise.
players.fantasyAdvice: Monitor from afar.

players.name: Sawyer Robertson
players.comparison: C.J. Stroud - Adrian O'Connell - Brad Kaaya - Connor Cook
players.notes: Operated in spread system with wide splits.
players.fantasyAdvice: Monitor from afar.

players.name: Joe Fagnano
players.comparison: Joe Burrow - Will Grier
players.notes: Elements that could help him emerge as viable NFL backup within 2-3 years. Monitor annually because he might become viable down the line.
players.fantasyAdvice: Monitor from afar but monitor annually for a few years because he might become a viable backup.

players.name: Haynes King
players.comparison: Easton Stick - Brennan Armstrong
players.notes: Good college QB. Athletic, tough, savvy enough for Saturday game. On cusp of Reserve tier.
players.fantasyAdvice: Monitor from afar.

players.name: Drew Allar
players.comparison: Blaine Gabbert
players.notes: Acclaimed high school prospect who failed to emerge as expected. Below-average accuracy per RSP charting.
players.fantasyAdvice: Monitor from afar. Will have a niche of supporters because he looks like a prototypical QB.

players.name: Behren Morton
players.comparison: Case Keenum - Zach Wilson
players.notes: Spread offense QB. Accurate to shallow zones. Limited success in intermediate/deep game.
players.fantasyAdvice: Monitor from afar.

players.name: Jeremiyah Love
players.comparison: Alvin Kamara - Reggie Bush/Tony Pollard - C.J. Spiller
players.notes: Top RB prospect. All-Pro potential per Waldman. On cusp of Franchise tier. Dynamic mover compared to Alvin Kamara. For many, Love is the top prospect among skill players in this class. Note: TheLeague's 0.25 RB PPR diminishes his receiving value vs RSP baseline, but his rushing talent is elite.
players.fantasyAdvice: For many, Love is the top prospect among the skill players in this class.

players.name: Emmett Johnson
players.comparison: Dalvin Cook - Devonta Freeman - Duke Johnson - Jordan James
players.notes: Pre-draft trajectory reminds Waldman of Harold Fannin's. Gained acclaim during the season. Underrated talent.
players.fantasyAdvice: Might be available in 3rd or 4th rounds of rookie drafts ahead of NFL Draft.

players.name: Demond Claiborne
players.comparison: Marshall Faulk/LeSean McCoy - James Cook/Charlie Garner - Steve Slaton
players.notes: Has some LeSean 'Shady' McCoy to his game. High ceiling. Weight is a concern. Second-round pick in rookie drafts regardless of roster size.
players.fantasyAdvice: Second-round pick in rookie drafts regardless of roster size. Draft capital will dictate post-draft value.

players.name: Myles Montgomery
players.comparison: Cadillac Williams - Kenny Gainwell - Jeff Wilson
players.notes: RSP's pick as the MOST UNDERRATED skill talent in the 2026 class. Hula Bowl standout. Likely UDFA -- add from waivers in shallow leagues.
players.fantasyAdvice: Likely a UDFA. Add from waivers in shallow leagues after the draft. In deep leagues, late-round stash.

players.name: Nicholas Singleton
players.comparison: Jonathan Brooks - Melvin Gordon - Jeff Wilson
players.notes: Burst on the college scene as one of the top recruited backs. Consider a second- or third-round pick in rookie drafts before landing spot.
players.fantasyAdvice: Second- or third-round pick in rookie drafts.

players.name: Jadarian Price
players.comparison: Terrell Davis - Jonathan Franklin - Ty Chandler - George Holani
players.notes: Talented sidekick to a star (Love) in a backfield. Ceiling is as high as RBs 2-5. Gets pre-draft bump from media as Love's backup.
players.fantasyAdvice: Ranking slightly lower than RBs 2-5 but ceiling is as high. Worth a mid-round selection.

players.name: Robert Henry Jr.
players.comparison: Aaron Jones - Eric Metcalf - Nyheim Hines
players.notes: De'Von Achane, Aaron Jones, and James Cook are examples of top-performing NFL backs who compare. Underrated talent from small school.
players.fantasyAdvice: Mid-round pick in large leagues (30+ roster spots), late-round in smaller formats.

players.name: Jamal Haynes
players.comparison: Chris Johnson - James Cook
players.notes: Arrived at GT as a slot receiver. Didn't even play RB initially. Late-round pick worth before NFL Draft. Could earn higher value after Draft.
players.fantasyAdvice: Worth a late-round pick before the NFL Draft. Could earn higher value after landing spot.

players.name: TJ Harden
players.comparison: Arian Foster - DeShaun Foster - Ryan Torain
players.notes: Could appeal to teams moving to heavy base sets. Well-built power runner with better speed than expected. Likely UDFA worth cheap investment.
players.fantasyAdvice: Likely a UDFA. Worth a cheap investment at the end of your drafts.

players.name: Davon Booth
players.comparison: Dalvin Cook/J.K. Dobbins - Khalil Herbert/Kyle Monangai
players.notes: Another sleeper in this RB class who could become productive part of a committee. If earns early Day Three draft capital, consider in mid-rounds.
players.fantasyAdvice: If earns early Day Three NFL Draft capital, consider in mid-rounds in large leagues.

players.name: J'Mari Taylor
players.comparison: Ray Davis - Sincere McCormick
players.notes: Likely career backup but the caliber of backup that could have 1-2 strong seasons as a starter.
players.fantasyAdvice: Mid-round consideration in a draft with 7-10 picks. End of round value in smaller drafts.

players.name: Le'Veon Moss
players.comparison: Bilal Powell - James Jackson/Ke'Shawn Vaughn
players.notes: Running style makes you want to love his game. Plays with an edge. Could develop into committee back.
players.fantasyAdvice: Mid-round selection in fantasy drafts. Could develop into a committee back.

players.name: Roman Hemby
players.notes: High floor of potential but lower ceiling than some of the others ranked below him.
players.fantasyAdvice: Monitor from afar despite the high floor. May change if earns draft capital.

players.name: Mike Washington Jr.
players.comparison: Corey Dillon/Chris Carson/Rashard Mendenhall - Carlos Hyde
players.notes: Two poles of thought pre-draft. High ceiling. Big, physical runner. Better opportunity than ranking suggests because of his athletic profile.
players.fantasyAdvice: Will be valued as one of the top 3-5 backs in rookie drafts. Better opportunity than ranking suggests.

players.name: Leshon Williams
players.comparison: James Robinson - Ke-Shawn Vaughn/Tashard Choice - Javorius Allen
players.notes: Underrated. Will likely be UDFA or late-round draft pick. Worth a late-round pick.
players.fantasyAdvice: Viable late-round pick who will likely be UDFA or 6th-7th round.

players.name: Kaytron Allen
players.comparison: Josh Jacobs - Jonas Gray/Jonathan Williams
players.notes: Should be a stalwart RB3/RB4 on several rosters. Late-round selection.
players.fantasyAdvice: Late-round selection in most leagues.

players.name: Kaelon Black
players.comparison: J.K. Dobbins - Sean Tucker - Jordan James
players.notes: Smart runner with cutback ability who attacks downhill with ferocity for his size. Patience play but could pop with training camp injuries.
players.fantasyAdvice: Patience play, but could pop if training camp injuries clear a path.

players.name: Desmond Reid
players.comparison: Tarik Cohen - J.J. Taylor
players.notes: Is Reid the next Bucky Irving? Tiny but dynamic. Limited upside ceiling but if league awards return points, mid-round pick.
players.fantasyAdvice: If league awards points for returns, mid-round pick. Otherwise, best as a late-round stash.

players.name: Rahsul Faison
players.comparison: Bilal Powell/Le'Veon Moss - James Jackson/Ke'Shawn Vaughn
players.notes: Versatile and patient runner who works tight creases. Wide range of moves. Late-round dart throw.
players.fantasyAdvice: Late-round pick who could earn mid-round value if NFL gives better draft capital.

players.name: Jamarion Miller
players.comparison: Isaiah Pacheco - Ke'Shawn Vaughn
players.notes: Speedster with questionable acceleration. Was slow this year due to lingering injury. Late-round dart throw in larger formats.
players.fantasyAdvice: Late-round dart-throw in larger formats.

players.name: Coleman Bennett
players.comparison: Bilal Powell/Le'Veon Moss - James Jackson/Ke'Shawn Vaughn
players.notes: Decisive tight-crease runner. Late-round pick in deep leagues with at least 30 roster spots and practice squads.
players.fantasyAdvice: Late-round pick in deep leagues with at least 30 roster spots and practice squads.

players.name: Kejon Owens
players.comparison: J.K. Dobbins - Sean Tucker - Jordan James/Kaelon Black
players.notes: Won't have NFL Draft capital but RSP has him ranked 22nd at the position. Monitor from afar.
players.fantasyAdvice: Monitor from afar until there's reason to add.

players.name: Adam Randall
players.comparison: Najee Harris - Chris Brooks - Sewo Olonilua
players.notes: Big and fast for his size. Earns buzz for potential to become excellent pro. Worth stash in deep leagues.
players.fantasyAdvice: Worth a stash in deep leagues with practice squads -- at least 30 roster spots.

players.name: Dean Connors
players.comparison: Clinton Portis - Aaron Jones - Jacory Croskey-Merritt - Tevin Coleman
players.notes: Skilled pass-catcher and space player. Success as a gap runner. Could begin career as contributor.
players.fantasyAdvice: Monitor from afar until we see draft capital.

players.name: Chip Trayanum
players.school: Ohio State / Arizona State / Kentucky / Toledo
players.comparison: Jonathan Taylor - Quinshon Judkins/Chris Carson/Isaiah Crowell
players.notes: Profiled in 2025 without an NFL-grade grade. Improved significantly. Monitor from afar but might be worth adding in 1-2 years.
players.fantasyAdvice: Monitor from afar. Might be worth adding in 1-2 years if he earns an opportunity.

players.name: Jaden Nixon
players.comparison: Kyren Williams - Chris Thompson - Tyler Goodson - Tyler Badie/LeVante Bellamy
players.notes: Ashton Jeanty backed up Jaden Nixon in high school. Fascinating parallel. Return specialist potential.
players.fantasyAdvice: Monitor from afar in most formats. Consider if league awards return points.

players.name: Kentrel Bullock
players.comparison: Jeff Wilson/Jake Funk - Nay'Quan Wright
players.notes: Low center of gravity. Strong contact balance against box defenders.
players.fantasyAdvice: Monitor from afar.

players.name: Zach Palmer-Smith
players.comparison: Jordan Howard - Bam Knight - Zamir White
players.notes: Decisive and powerful runner who runs through contact and pushes piles.
players.fantasyAdvice: Monitor from afar.

players.name: Jonah Coleman
players.comparison: Maurice Jones-Drew - Travis Henry - C.J. Anderson/Peyton Barber - Miyan Williams
players.notes: Nimble back with low center of gravity. Patient zone runner. Consensus may consider him draftable but RSP is less bullish.
players.fantasyAdvice: Monitor from afar despite peers considering him draftable.

players.name: Freddie Brock IV
players.notes: Shifty runner with decisive and patient decision making.
players.fantasyAdvice: Monitor from afar.

players.name: Al-Jay Henderson
players.comparison: Ryan Grant - J.R. Redmond
players.notes: Starter size and enough explosion to threaten secondary. On cusp of Contributor tier.
players.fantasyAdvice: Monitor from afar.

players.name: Seth McGowan
players.comparison: Omarion Hampton/Tyler Allgeier - Kevin Harris/Jonathan Williams
players.notes: Higher ranking elsewhere based on size and timed speed. On cusp of Contributor tier.
players.fantasyAdvice: Late-round consideration in leagues with 35+ roster spots and practice squad.

players.name: Carnell Tate
players.comparison: Randy Moss - Justin Jefferson
players.notes: FRANCHISE-CALIBER TALENT. Highest score in the entire 2026 class at any position (92.3). Compared to Randy Moss and Justin Jefferson. Immediate production and leadership anchor. The clear WR1 in this class.
players.fantasyAdvice: Consensus top WR prospect. First-round lock in rookie drafts.

players.name: Makai Lemon
players.comparison: Amon-Ra St. Brown - John Metchie III
players.notes: Starter grade. Expected to remain early-round prospect through draft season. Amon-Ra St. Brown comparison is elite for fantasy.
players.fantasyAdvice: Will remain an early-round prospect through draft season.

players.name: Malachi Fields
players.comparison: Nico Collins - Tee Higgins
players.notes: Starter grade. Top-10 rookie pick in most formats. Excellent size/speed combination. Nico Collins comp is a great dynasty profile.
players.fantasyAdvice: Top-10 rookie pick in most formats.

players.name: KC Concepcion
players.comparison: Steve Smith II (NYG) - Khalil Shakir - Jermaine Burton (immaturity)
players.notes: Savvy receiver who can excel at flanker or slot. Delivers in the return game. First-round value in rookie drafts.
players.fantasyAdvice: First-round value in rookie drafts. Would rather see him slide to late first but worth early consideration.

players.name: Skyler Bell
players.comparison: Ja'Marr Chase - Laveranues Coles - Khalil Shakir - Braylon Sanders
players.notes: The exception to the rule. High ceiling. Ja'Marr Chase comp on the high end is extraordinary. Low-risk only if you agree with RSP's assessment of his clap-attacking technique.
players.fantasyAdvice: If you want low-risk, his clap-attacking rules him out. If you agree with RSP's assessment of his ceiling, first-round value.

players.name: Jordyn Tyson
players.comparison: Chris Godwin - Rashaun Woods
players.notes: Considered one of the best WR prospects by much of the industry. Boom-bust volatility. First-round pick in rookie drafts.
players.fantasyAdvice: First-round pick in rookie drafts. Boom-bust volatility embedded.

players.name: Chris Brazzell II
players.comparison: George Pickens - Chris Henry/Ja'Kobi Lane - Justin Hunter
players.notes: Winner of 'Who Looks the Most Like George Pickens Contest.' High ceiling with George Pickens-like traits.
players.fantasyAdvice: First-round fantasy selection in rookie drafts unless unanticipated event occurs.

players.name: Omar Cooper Jr.
players.comparison: Roddy White - Quintez Cephus
players.notes: Could become a Deebo Samuel clone but that means he struggled to become a complete outside receiver. Worth second-round consideration.
players.fantasyAdvice: If Cooper becomes a Deebo clone, he struggled at outside receiver. If he falls to second round, worth the risk.

players.name: Chris Bell
players.comparison: A.J. Brown - Michael Westbrook - Quincy Enunwa - Treylon Burks - Jonathan Mingo
players.notes: Equal fear about passing or drafting. A.J. Brown high-end comp is tantalizing. Wait for second round if falls.
players.fantasyAdvice: If falls to second round, worth the risk. Wait a year or two for development.

players.name: Elijah Sarratt
players.comparison: Michael Wilson - Josh Palmer - Bryan Edwards
players.notes: Well-built possession receiver. Safest option by RSP scoring among WRs 10-16 on the board.
players.fantasyAdvice: May be the safest option among WRs 10-16. Worth second-round consideration.

players.name: Antonio Williams
players.comparison: Ladd McConkey - Jayden Reed
players.notes: Jayden Reed has been an RSP fave. Williams compares well. Worth second-round consideration and a potential bargain in third.
players.fantasyAdvice: Worth second-round consideration and potential bargain in third round.

players.name: Josh Cameron
players.comparison: A.J. Brown - Anquan Boldin - Mohamed Sanu
players.notes: Models game after Larry Fitzgerald. A.J. Brown/Anquan Boldin comp. Medium floor with WR3 potential.
players.fantasyAdvice: Cameron's floor as a potential WR3 isn't nothing. Worth a mid-round pick.

players.name: Germie Bernard
players.comparison: Steve Johnson - Allen Hurns - Tyler Johnson
players.notes: Medium floor with high ceiling. Not elite top 3-5 ceiling but solid starter potential.
players.fantasyAdvice: Medium floor with high ceiling. Worth a mid-round pick.

players.name: Denzel Boston
players.notes: Skilled boundary receiver with excellent acceleration for his size. Gets open early in routes. Quality pick in late first or early second rounds.
players.fantasyAdvice: Quality pick in late first half or early second rounds in rookie drafts.

players.name: J. Michael Sturdivant
players.comparison: Javon Walker/Michael Wilson - Braylon Edwards - Seth Williams/David Terrell
players.notes: Deceptive evaluation. Role at Florida didn't showcase true skills. Has potential to become a starter. First of several receivers with potential that draft capital could dictate where he lands.
players.fantasyAdvice: First of several receivers with starter potential. Mid-round consideration.

players.name: Zachariah Branch
players.comparison: DeVonta Smith - Jordan Addison - Samie Parker - Dede Westbrook
players.notes: Boom-bust profile. Explosive with big-play ability. Volatile ADP range pre-draft. Draft capital could dictate fantasy value post-draft.
players.fantasyAdvice: Volatile ADP range pre-draft. Post-draft capital could dictate value.

players.name: Kevin Coleman Jr.
players.school: Missouri / Mississippi State / Louisville / Jackson State
players.comparison: Jayden Reed - Jalen Nailor - Whop Philyor
players.notes: Reminds Waldman of Jayden Reed and a former Michigan State teammate. Consider in mid-rounds before NFL Draft.
players.fantasyAdvice: Consider in mid-rounds before NFL Draft. Draft capital will refine value.

players.name: Eric McAlister
players.comparison: George Pickens - Chris Henry/Ja'Kobi Lane - Justin Hunter
players.notes: Big play threat with excellent length, good speed, and acrobatic knack. Will likely have lower NFL Draft capital than his skills and ceiling might suggest.
players.fantasyAdvice: Will likely have lower NFL Draft capital than skills suggest. Buy low in mid-to-late rounds.

players.name: Ja'Kobi Lane
players.comparison: George Pickens - Chris Henry - Justin Hunter
players.notes: Can become primary receiver in the NFL -- possibly a star receiver. Details of his game grew as season progressed.
players.fantasyAdvice: Medium-floor, medium-ceiling option. There's consistently a role for tall receivers.

players.name: Deion Burks
players.comparison: Josh Downs/Eric Rivers/Jamison Crowder or DeVonta Smith - Jordan Addison
players.notes: Offers big plays from the slot and has potential to work as a flanker in NFL offense. The safe route is 500-700 yards as big-play slot.
players.fantasyAdvice: Safe route is to value as big-play slot receiver earning 500-700 yards.

players.name: Bryce Lance
players.comparison: A.J. Green - George Pickens
players.notes: Trey Lance's younger brother. A.J. Green high-end comp. Has promise to make a name for himself. Must-draft in leagues with at least 35 roster spots.
players.fantasyAdvice: Must-draft in leagues with 35+ roster spots. For smaller rosters, late-round stash.

players.name: Ted Hurst
players.comparison: A.J. Green - Tee Higgins
players.notes: Development could go in two distinct directions. Worth mid-to-late round consideration. NFL will draft him based on combine performance.
players.fantasyAdvice: Worth mid-to-late pick in rookie drafts.

players.name: Malik Benson
players.comparison: Chris Olave - Kyle Williams
players.notes: Speedster capable of stretching defense. Needs to refine for NFL-level consistency. Worth stash for at least a year.
players.fantasyAdvice: Worth consideration as late-round pick you intend on stashing for at least a year.

players.name: Junior Vandeross III
players.comparison: Wan'Dale Robinson - Greg Dortch - Keke Coutee
players.notes: Slot receiver with big-play potential. Didn't earn NFL Combine invitation. Sleeper if earns draft capital inside Day 3.
players.fantasyAdvice: Monitor from afar until draft capital. If earns capital inside top 160, worth a mid-round stash.

players.name: Jordan Hudson
players.comparison: Reggie Wayne - Jerry Jeudy
players.notes: High-floor, medium-ceiling possession receiver with enough speed to stretch the field. Better ranking than several players above him by others.
players.fantasyAdvice: High-floor, medium-ceiling option.

players.name: Eli Stowers
players.comparison: Trey McBride - Isaiah Likely - Harrison Bryant - Farrod Green
players.notes: Former No.12 high school QB. Converted to TE after shoulder injury. Classic move TE. On cusp of Starter tier. More complete route runner and pass-catcher than Trigg or Sadiq. Fewer lapses with strengths of his game. Full PPR in TheLeague (1.0 for TE) makes him match RSP valuation.
players.fantasyAdvice: Technically an instant starter with RSP grade but still on cusp of rotational starter. Worth early-round pick in TE premium.

players.name: Michael Trigg
players.comparison: Jordan Reed - Gerald Everett/Isaiah Likely
players.notes: Might be the riskiest TE in the draft. Flair for the spectacular but difficulties with the fundamentals. More athletic upside than Stowers. Risk/reward profile.
players.fantasyAdvice: Safest path is to wait for NFL Draft landing spot. High-risk, high-reward.

players.name: Kenyon Sadiq
players.comparison: Brock Bowers - Delanie Walker - Noah Gray - Brevin Jordan
players.notes: Lit up the NFL Combine with speed, vertical leaping, and bench press. Explosive athlete. Brock Bowers high-end comp is extraordinary. More athletic upside than Stowers. Late second round in 1.5 PPR, mid-round consideration in standard PPR.
players.fantasyAdvice: Late second round in 1.5 PPR, mid-round in standard PPR.

players.name: Justin Joly
players.comparison: Jonnu Smith - Tommy Tremble
players.notes: One of Waldman's favorites in this TE class. Elite pass catcher with a route game that can become special. Full PPR TE in TheLeague makes him very valuable.
players.fantasyAdvice: Worth a late-round pick in deep PPR formats and mid-round pick in TE premium.

players.name: Carsen Ryan
players.comparison: Jake Ferguson
players.notes: Didn't receive NFL Combine invitation -- most puzzling development for Waldman. Underrated. Jake Ferguson comp is a high floor.
players.fantasyAdvice: Should be available in final rounds unless other RSP subscribers target him.

players.name: Sam Roush
players.comparison: Rob Gronkowski - Brandon Pettigrew - Brevyn Spann-Ford
players.notes: Runs effective routes but in limited range. Rob Gronkowski high-end comp but limited matchup threat. Increase value if lands with multiple-TE scheme.
players.fantasyAdvice: If joins team with multiple-TE scheme, increase value. Worth a stash.

players.name: Joshua Cuevas
players.comparison: Noah Gray - Brevin Jordan
players.notes: Makings of good backup TE who can contribute off bench. Rankings are relative; DoT scores are not. Can contribute and might even become productive starter.
players.fantasyAdvice: Rankings relative; DoT scores are not. Can contribute and might become productive.

players.name: Riley Nowakowski
players.comparison: Josiah Deguara - Hunter Bryant - Charlie Taumoepeau
players.notes: Walk-on at Wisconsin. Converted from linebacker. Exceptional case of high situational value. Underrated. On cusp of Contributor tier.
players.fantasyAdvice: Exceptional case of a prospect with high situational value.

players.name: Max Klare
players.comparison: Cade Stover
players.notes: Converted QB. Lacks muscle memory with areas of new position that should improve. Cade Stover comp is strong for TheLeague. On cusp of Rotational Starter.
players.fantasyAdvice: Has boom-bust due to inconsistencies with hands. Monitor development.

players.name: Jack Endries
players.comparison: Trey McBride - Zach Ertz - Oscar Delp - Charlie Woerner
players.notes: Lot of Zach Ertz to his game. Works middle and flats with sophistication. High-floor/low-ceiling for fantasy. On cusp of Rotational Starter.
players.fantasyAdvice: High-floor/low-ceiling receiver for fantasy. Fast enough to stretch the field.

players.name: Joe Royer
players.comparison: Jermichael Finley - Gunnar Helm - Jake Butt
players.notes: Began career at Ohio State but couldn't crack lineup. Replacement option alongside Klare. On cusp of Rotational Starter.
players.fantasyAdvice: Will have pockets of fans who value him higher than likely value.

players.name: Oscar Delp
players.comparison: Trey McBride - Zach Ertz - Charlie Woerner
players.notes: A lot to like but falls short of love. On cusp of Rotational Starter. Worth mid-to-late stash.
players.fantasyAdvice: Worthwhile mid-to-late stash depending on scoring system.

players.name: Will Kacmarek
players.comparison: A.J. Barner - John Bates - Brevynn Spann-Ford - Luke Farrell
players.notes: Big-bodied sidekick to Max Klare at Ohio State. High-floor, low-ceiling prospect. On cusp of Rotational Starter.
players.fantasyAdvice: High-floor, low-ceiling fantasy prospect. Likely tied to blocking role.

players.name: Lake McRee
players.comparison: Sam LaPorta - Harold Fannin - Oronde Gadsden II
players.notes: Route-running and receiving are the assets. Sam LaPorta high-end comp is excellent for dynasty. Worth consideration in 1.0+ PPR formats.
players.fantasyAdvice: Worth consideration in 1.0+ PPR scoring formats in large-roster leagues with practice squads.
